title: Vaccinium glandulosum
intro: Vaccinium glandulosum, also known as the glandular blueberry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae) that is native to eastern Asia. The plant is rich in health benefits, with its extracts and compounds exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various diseases, including cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium glandulosum include anthocyanins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Vaccinium glandulosum is typically used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, which are valued for their potential to promote cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
taxonomy: Vaccinium glandulosum is a species of shrub in the heath family, Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Vaccinium, which includes blueberries and cranberries. The species is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. Vaccinium glandulosum is classified as a flowering plant, with small white or pink flowers that grow in clusters. It is a member of the order Ericales, which consists of various flowering plants often found in acidic soils.
uses: Vaccinium glandulosum, also known as the glandular blueberry, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems are used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is thought to help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In some cultures, Vaccinium glandulosum is used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of its medicinal uses.

constituents: Vaccinium glandulosum, a species of blueberry, is known to contain various active constituents. These constituents include anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants responsible for the plant's blue color. Vaccinium glandulosum also contains other bioactive compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have been linked to potential health benefits. The plant's active constituents may also include vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and manganese.
